figures.caption: Figure 1: Modal Logical Neural Networks for Finance. Left: Kripke semantics with possible worlds (temporal: t, t + 1; stress: crash world wc). Middle: Two learning modes—(A) Deductive: fixed accessibility R (e.g., time flow), (B) Inductive: learned Aθ (e.g., trust networks). Modal operators □ (necessity: all worlds) and ♢ (possibility: ≥1 world) enable differentiable logical reasoning. Right: Four financial applications leveraging these modes for compliance, surveillance, risk management, and interpretability.
